title: Qlog Internal Capture Verification
scope: Verify the internal qlog capture coordinator, session-scoped and process-wide registration, immutable snapshot creation, non-blocking off-thread dispatch, and the bounded-by-default backlog policy. This slice deliberately excludes concrete file or streaming sink implementations.
verification_method: Specification conformance checks and focused unit tests with concurrency and ordering assertions.
preconditions: The qlog SpecTrace artifacts validate successfully under the repository's JSON validation script.
The internal capture implementation exists in `Incursa.Qlog`.
A test double observer or sink is available to inspect ordering and frozen payload behavior.
procedure: Run scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 over the qlog canonical artifacts.
Confirm the public API baseline keeps capture and sink coordination internal while exposing the retained qlog model and serializer entry points.
Confirm that mutating a source `QlogEvent` after capture does not alter dispatched snapshots.
Confirm that session-scoped and process-wide observers both receive the expected frozen events.
Confirm that the current implementation preserves per-session ordering while dispatch work stays off the transport callback path without turning ordering into a public guarantee.
Confirm that the default dispatch options stay bounded and compute a recommended capacity from available memory heuristics.
Confirm that a bounded queue drops newly captured events instead of blocking when an observer falls behind.
Confirm that the internal unbounded override accepts the same workload without bounded-queue drops.
expected_result: The capture pipeline dispatches frozen qlog event snapshots through the internal coordinator without exposing public sink contracts, defaults to a bounded non-blocking backlog policy, and still allows an internal unbounded override for explicit high-memory scenarios.
